About

Julia Meryl Meyerson

Junior at Carnegie Mellon studying Mechanical Engineering with a minor in Robotics. I'm drawn to problems where mechanical design and software intersect - autonomous robots, assistive exoskeletons, precision mechatronic systems.

I've done research on robotic exoskeletons at CMU's Robotics Institute and on autonomous UGVs for electrical substation inspection, which was published in the ASME Journal of Mechanisms and Robotics. Past internships at Caterpillar (fuel system mechatronics) and RST Automation; this summer joining Symbotic's mechatronics team.

Outside class I TA Mechanics I and II, co-lead a 100+ person team building Spring Carnival booths, and race carbon fiber gravity racers on Apex Buggy.

Research

CMU MetaMobility Lab
2024–2025
Undergraduate Researcher · Robotics Institute
📍 Pittsburgh, PA
  • Conducted research on robotic hip and ankle exoskeleton development for populations with motor impairments
  • Fabricated carbon fiber hip exoskeleton components; coordinated parts procurement, layup, and post-processing; authored lab fabrication documentation
  • Redesigned hip exoskeleton thigh bar, developing sliding and fixed designs in carbon fiber and aluminum with a focus on machinability
  • Designed, iterated, and began manufacturing a lightweight ankle exoskeleton designed to interface with hip and knee exoskeletons

Projects

Research & Class Projects

Research · CMU MetaMobility Lab · Robotics Institute
Assistive Exoskeletons - Hip & Ankle

Research on robotic hip and ankle exoskeleton development at CMU's MetaMobility Lab. Fabricated carbon fiber hip components, redesigned hip thigh bar in CF and aluminum, and designed a lightweight ankle exoskeleton to interface with the lab's broader exoskeleton system.

Carbon FiberSolidWorks Biomechanics3D Printing Assistive TechMechanical Design
🏎️
F1Tenth Photos
Class Project · Robotics
F1Tenth Autonomous Racing

Built and programmed a 1/10-scale autonomous race car. Implemented LiDAR-based perception, motion planning, and real-time control for high-speed track navigation.

ROS2Python LiDARPath Planning
Research · Stevens RFAL · Published ASME JMR
Substation Inspection UGVs

Autonomous UGVs for navigating and inspecting electrical substations. Built the full stack - LiDAR navigation, computer vision for barcode recognition, OCR for breaker reading, robotic manipulators, and Gazebo simulation. Published in ASME Journal of Mechanisms and Robotics.

ROS / GazeboLiDAR Computer VisionOCR PythonASME Publication
Class Project · Design
Assistive Potato Masher

Motorized assistive potato masher for users with limited hand strength or dexterity (cerebral palsy, wrist tendonitis). Self-balancing with a linear actuator for mashing force, bowl-mounted frame, ball joint with joystick control, and removable masher end for cleaning.

Assistive TechLinear Actuator 3D PrintingElectromechanicalUser Research
Class Project · Design & Manufacturing
Hiking Chair / Backpack

Dual-function hiking chair/backpack with adjustable telescoping legs. Designed through user research to low/mid-fidelity prototypes and a final functional build, validated with FEA and hand calculations.

FEA / AnsysManual Machining PrototypingUX Research
Spring Carnival · SDC Booth

Booth Builds

Every spring, CMU's Spring Carnival has student organizations build and decorate full one-story structures. As Game & Prop Chair, I design and fabricate the themed games and props. Below is work from both years - full booth shots and individual props.

Wall-E - 2025
Spring Carnival 2025

"Repair Wall-E" - players reconnect batteries to checkpoints through a magnetic maze to restore Wall-E's power, tracked on an Arduino-controlled LCD display. Designed in SolidWorks; fabricated with laser cutting, 3D printing, and custom circuitry.

Full game board - Repair Wall-E
Maze detail with magnetic wire paths
Arduino-controlled solar charge display
SDC Booth Wall-E - Spring Carnival 2025
Beauty & the Beast - 2024
Spring Carnival 2024

Enchanted castle themed booth with custom props, structural woodwork, themed decorations, and immersive lighting.

Miscellaneous

Other Work

Robot Hackathons

Two award-winning hackathon robots at CMU. Red Robot Hackathon Judges' Choice - PID line-following with manipulators. Garbage Hackathon Best Use of E-waste - creative locomotion from repurposed electronics.

PIDMechatronicsArduino
"Wall-E" Social Robot

Tracked robot with facial recognition, autonomous operation, and remote control. Built for CMU's Humanoids class.

Computer VisionMechatronicsFabrication
Other MechE Class Projects

Gear train mechanism, Mobot (maze-navigating autonomous robot), 4-bar linkage FEA, hand-cranked flashlight, 5g acrylic bar holding 40 lb, brittle failure analysis, and more.
